prompt:
We have an audit discrepancy in the account service. Email changes and deactivations both succeed, but the events arriving downstream do not have one invariant shape: one path loses the middleware actor/request identity and uses a different action name. A separate reconciliation job calls the same command interface and currently produces no audit trail at all. Fixing each HTTP handler independently is not acceptable; this repository has an ownership boundary specifically so every caller gets the invariant once.

Please orient through internal/httpapi, request context middleware, the account command interface/service, and the generated mock before changing production code. Successful email changes and deactivations must each emit exactly one canonical event after persistence succeeds. Failed mutations emit nothing. Handler response behavior, context propagation, batch use of the interface, store error identity, and the generated mock contract must remain intact.

Acceptance is `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...`. The test, go.mod, and generated mock are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace handler calls through middleware and interfaces to consolidate an audit invariant at the mutation-owning service boundary
full_distill.baseline_evidence: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... fails because HTTP deactivation records account.disabled as system with no request ID, while direct batch commands record no event
full_distill.success_contract: all successful account mutations emit one canonical post-persistence event with middleware identity for HTTP and batch callers, while failures emit none and handlers remain interface-only adapters
adversarial_factors: removing or correcting only handler events leaves the protected batch caller without audit evidence
adding service events without removing handler-owned emission creates duplicates that the suite counts
a generated command mock and rejecting audit sink prove handlers only forward context and do not own the invariant
both command methods are exercised with handler, store, and audit-sink failures so asymmetric error swallowing or pre-persistence emission is rejected
